title: Ultraviolet light emitter
abstract: A light emitting assembly comprises an ultraviolet (UV) light emitting diode (LED) coupled to a UV reflector. The reflector has a three-dimensional shape spanning an angular range between 0Â° and 180Â°about a principal reflector axis of the reflector. The reflector is smooth and continuous across its entire surface. The UV-LED is directed towards a reflective surface of the reflector instead of towards the fluid to be treated. A central ray of the UV-LED impinges directly on the reflective surface of the reflector.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An ultraviolet (UV) radiation emitting assembly comprising: one or more UV-LED(s) having a principal optical axis that is central to the direction of radiation emission from the one or more UV-LED(s); and a reflector located adjacent to the UV-LED(s) and having a three-dimensional shape defined by rotating a smooth and continuous curve having two end points about a principal reflector axis over a reflector angle Q, where the reflector angle Q is in a range of 120Â°-300Â°, the principal reflector axis intersecting the curve at one of the two end points and at no other points on the curve; wherein the principal optical axis of the UV-LED(s) is directed toward the reflector. 2. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 1 or any other claim herein wherein the curve is a portion of a parabola. 3. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 1 or any other claim herein wherein the curve is a portion of an ellipse. 4. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 1 or any other claim herein wherein the curve is a portion of a circular arc. 5. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 1 or any other claim herein wherein the curve is a straight line. 6. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 1 or any other claim herein wherein the curve comprises a first portion that is a portion of a parabola and a second portion that is a portion of an ellipse. 7. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 6 or any other claim herein wherein the reflector comprises material suitable for predominantly specular reflection of UV light (e.g. light having wavelengths in a range of 250- 300nm). 8. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 7 or any other claim herein wherein the principal optical axis of each UV-LED forms an angle a with the principal reflector axis, the angle a in a range of 45 Â°- 135Â°. 9. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 7 or any other claim herein wherein the principal optical axis of each UV-LED forms an angle a with the principal reflector axis, the angle a in a range of 85Â°-95Â°. 10. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 9 or any other claim herein wherein the one or more UV-LED(s) comprise a plurality of UV-LEDs arranged symmetrically about a focal point of the reflector. 11. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to claim 10 or any other claim herein wherein the plurality of UV-LEDs is located on the principal reflector axis. 12. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 9 or any other claim herein wherein the one or more UV-LED(s) comprise one UV-LED located on the principal reflector axis at a focal point of the reflector. 13. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 12 or any other claim herein wherein each UV-LED is located within a circle centered at the principal reflector axis and having a radius of 1/5 of a distance between the reflector and the principal reflector axis measured at an axial location of the UV-LED. 14. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 13 or any other claim herein wherein each UV-LED is located within a distance of 5 times a dimension of the UV-LED of the focal point of the reflector. 15. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 14 or any other claim herein wherein the three-dimensional shape is shaped to capture radiation emitted from the one or more UV-LED(s) from at least 67% of an effective emission angle F over which the UV-LED(s) emits radiation. 16. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 15 or any other claim herein wherein the three-dimensional shape is shaped to capture radiation emitted from the one or more UV-LED(s) over a emission angle F greater than F=50 Â® . 17. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 16 or any other claim herein comprising an optical sensor located adjacent to the reflector for measuring radiation emitted by the radiation emitting assembly. 18. A UV radiation emitting assembly according to any one of claims 1 to 16 or any other claim herein wherein the reflector is fabricated at least partially by a thermally conductive material and is shaped for dissipating heat generated by the UV-LED(s). 19. Use of the UV radiation emitting assembly of any of claims 1 to 18 or any other claim herein for a surface disinfection application. 20. Use of the UV radiation emitting assembly of any of claims 1 to 18 or any other claim herein for a flowing water disinfection application. 21 . Use according to claim 20 wherein the reflector is shaped to collimate radiation emitted from the UV-LED(s) such that the collimated direction is generally parallel (within a solid angle of 15 e ) with an average flow direction of the water in the flowing water disinfection application. 22. Use of the UV radiation emitting assembly of any of claims 1 to 18 or any other claim herein for a still water disinfection application. 23. An ultraviolet (UV) radiation emitting assembly comprising: one or more UV-LED(s) having a principal optical axis that is central to the direction of radiation emission from the one or more UV-LED(s); and a reflector located adjacent to the UV-LED(s) and having a three-dimensional shape defined by rotating a smooth and continuous curve having two end points 

 about a principal reflector axis over a reflector angle Q, where the reflector angle Q is is less than 360Â°, the principal reflector axis intersecting the curve at one of the two end points and at no other points on the curve; wherein the principal optical axis of the UV-LED(s) is directed toward the reflector; and wherein the three-dimensional shape is shaped to capture radiation emitted from the one or more UV-LED(s) from at least 67% of an effective emission angle F over which the UV-LED(s) emits radiation. 24.  endpoint and a second endpoint, the first end point intersected by the principal reflector axis; wherein for at least two such axial cross-section planes, the corresponding smooth and continuous cross-section curves are different from one another; wherein the defined three-dimensional shape varies smoothly between the cross-section curves; and wherein the principal optical axis of the UV-LED(s) is directed towards the reflector. 46.
